Empires & Puzzles Zenuk'wa Review: 5-Star Legendary Holy Yellow Hero
Zenuk'wa brings a unique ailment-punishing mechanic to the Beach Party, but does this two-headed ogre have the speed to compete? We dive into the stats and strategies.
Zenuk'wa Review: The Two-Headed Ice Cream Enthusiast
Zenuk'wa, The Conjoined Confectioner: A Slow Burn of Ailments and Ice Cream
Zenuk'wa is a 5-star Legendary Holy hero from the

event, introduced in mid-2024. As a Wizard class hero, they bring a rather unique—and frankly, bizarre—aesthetic to the battlefield. Imagine a two-headed ogre enjoying a summer vacation with ice cream in hand, and you're pretty much there.

speed ailment punisher. They are designed to synergize with teams that flood the enemy with status effects, turning those debuffs into cold, hard damage. However, in a world of blistering fast snipers and game-ending tanks, bringing a slow, ice-cream-eating ogre to a sword fight is a bold choice. Is it a sweet victory or a brain freeze waiting to happen? Let's find out.

The artwork is... certainly memorable. It captures the "Beach Party" theme with a level of grotesquerie that is rare in Empires & Puzzles. We have a two-headed female ogre character, clad in beachwear, enthusiastically devouring ice cream. It's colorful, it's distinct, and it definitely stands out in a roster full of serious knights and dragons. Whether you find it charmingly quirky or visually off-putting is entirely subjective, but you can't deny they are having a better summer than the rest of us.

Looking at Zenuk'wa's stats in the context of early 2026, they are undeniably showing their age. With a base attack just under 1000 and LB2 stats reaching around 1224, they fall significantly behind modern powerhouses who are pushing 1300+ base and 1600+ LB2 attack stats.
Average to Low by modern standards.
Respectable bulk, allowing them to survive long enough to fire (hopefully).
Stat-wise, Zenuk'wa is firmly in the "outdated" territory. They rely entirely on their special skill mechanics rather than raw stat superiority to make an impact.
This skill is a roller coaster of "Okay..." and "Wait, what?".
speed is, to put it bluntly, terrible. Modern Fast heroes hit harder than this. If you are bringing Zenuk'wa for the direct damage, you are doing it wrong.
This is the meat of the skill. 225 damage
on the target. If you can stack 3 or 4 ailments on an enemy (Defense Down, Poison, Burn, Mana Slow), Zenuk'wa suddenly drops an extra ~675-900 damage nuke on them.
Immunity to ailments for 4 turns is excellent, especially with the healing kicker. It essentially turns enemy debuffs into small heals.

The speed. At Slow, by the time Zenuk'wa is ready to fire, your ailment casters might have already fired and their effects might be wearing off. Timing is everything here, and Slow speed makes timing incredibly difficult outside of Rush formats.
Let's break down the potential damage output.
A modern Average speed hit-3 hero like
deals 390%+. A modern Slow hero like
deals 475% to ALL enemies. Zenuk'wa is severely undertuned here.

Zenuk'wa is a "Win More" hero. If you have already successfully landed multiple ailments on the enemy team, you are likely winning anyway. Zenuk'wa just seals the deal. If you haven't landed ailments, Zenuk'wa does almost nothing.
Family Bonus, Passives, Aether Power & Unique Abilities
Reduces damage from DoT (Damage over Time) effects. It's... fine. It helps with survival against burn/poison heavy teams, but it's not a game-changer.
Deals minor Water damage over 2 turns. It's a nice little bonus to strip "Cleanse on damage" buffs, but the damage itself is negligible.
Very niche. Useful against specific hydro-themed heroes, but rarely a deciding factor.
. This is actually a solid choice for a Slow hero. It helps keep Zenuk'wa alive while waiting for those 12 tiles.
Even at release in mid-2024, Zenuk'wa was considered underwhelming. The community reaction was largely negative, citing the low damage multiplier for a Slow hero.
Power creep has not been kind. With 2025/2026 heroes introducing "Magic" and "Styx" speeds with massive damage numbers, Zenuk'wa's 275% hit feels like a 4-star skill.
Zenuk'wa is effectively a niche pick for
. In standard play, they are too slow and too low-impact to justify a roster spot over almost any modern hero.
Zenuk'wa is Slow. They need to survive to fire. If they die with full mana, they do 0 damage. This path ensures they live to eat their ice cream.
Lowers the impact of the initial hit, but the main damage comes from the fixed 225 damage per ailment anyway, which doesn't scale with Attack stat.
Maximizes the 275% hit and the DoT passive.
High risk of dying before firing.
For the few players using Zenuk'wa, go
. The "Punish" damage (225 per ailment) is fixed and does not depend on your attack stat, so keeping the hero alive is the priority.
With the right setup (lots of ailments), the damage potential is massive.
4 turns of immunity for self and nearby is a strong defensive buff.
Turns enemy ailment attempts into healing.
The single biggest flaw. In modern E&P, Slow heroes need to practically win the game on their own (like Alfrike or Ludwig) to be viable. Zenuk'wa does not.
Useless on their own. Requires teammates to land ailments first.
The 275% hit is laughably weak for a Slow 5-star.
Only affects nearby enemies, limiting the board control.
This is the only place Zenuk'wa shines. At Very Fast speed, you can fire early, block ailments, and punish enemies who are also firing fast.
Can be fun in map stages where you can stack items and special skills to see big numbers, but inefficient for competitive ranking.
You need heroes that apply ailments
Minions that apply ailments on hit are perfect. They stack ailments passively, setting up Zenuk'wa for a big smash.
Heroes that drop defense down, poison, or mana ailments.
Bera - C. Freya - Zenuk'wa - Healer - Sniper
Bera gives minions that poison and block minions (2 potential ailment sources). Zenuk'wa fires and punishes anyone with those ailments.
If the enemy cleanses your ailments before Zenuk'wa fires, you do no damage.
Resist the ailments, reducing Zenuk'wa's damage potential.
Any fast team will likely kill Zenuk'wa before they charge.

If you pulled Zenuk'wa and lack other 5-star Holy heroes, they can serve as a tank or flank in lower arenas solely due to their stats being better than 4-stars. However, do not prioritize them over solid Average/Fast heroes.

Unless you are a collector or want to have fun in Rush Wars with a specific ailment team, Zenuk'wa is not worth the Alpha Aethers or Master Emblems. There are simply better options for damage and support.
The community was... unkind to Zenuk'wa upon release.
"A hero, no you can't call that a hero. He’s the worst character in the game."
"Ice Cream Smash is a fitting name... because is it about as strong as an actual hit by ice cream."
Many players immediately flagged them as
, preferring to trade them for older, more reliable heroes.
(released in the same batch) was often brought up, noting how Mazoga was also underwhelming but at least Fast.

Zenuk'wa is a classic example of a "fun idea, poor execution." The concept of punishing ailments is great, but locking it behind Slow speed and a weak base hit makes it impractical for the current high-speed meta. They are a hero searching for a problem that other heroes solve better. If you max them, do it for the love of the wacky art or the specific Rush War niche, not for competitive dominance.
A frosty reception for a hero that melts under pressure.
Is Zenuk'wa worth the mats? We analyze this Beach Party hero's unique ailment-punishing mechanics, stats, and viability in the current Empires & Puzzles meta.
